jokes.text: Bad news, drivers. There's an overturned melon truck on the interstate. Oh, it's a mess. There's lots of rubber neck in gandmelon wrestling going on
jokes.setup: Traffic reporter giving standard traffic update
jokes.punchline: Malapropism 'rubber neck in gandmelon wrestling' instead of 'rubbernecking and melon wrestling'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that gets a chuckle but isn't particularly memorable

jokes.text: Hey, doughnuts! - Bart, there's one left, and it's mine.
jokes.setup: Homer notices doughnuts while driving and talking
jokes.punchline: Immediate possessive claim over the last donut, ignoring his driving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - food obsession trumps everything else

jokes.text: Cool your jets, man. We're comin'.
jokes.setup: School bus arriving and honking
jokes.punchline: Bart's overly casual response to authority
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's rebellious attitude

jokes.text: Oh! - Aw, Homer! - Aw, Dad! - Uh-oh, schoolbus!
jokes.setup: Homer grabbing for donut while driving causes crash
jokes.punchline: Family's resigned reactions to Homer's predictable behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons physical comedy with family's weary acceptance

jokes.text: Our number is 555-PAIN
jokes.setup: Psychology radio show giving their call-in number
jokes.punchline: Phone number spells PAIN instead of something comforting
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of expectations with excellent economy

jokes.text: Save your whining for when you're on the air, okay?
jokes.setup: Marge pouring her heart out about marital problems
jokes.punchline: Callous dismissal by phone screener
jokes.explanation: Sharp cynical humor about media exploitation

jokes.text: Next we have Marge. She's 34 and trapped in a loveless sham of a marriage.
jokes.setup: Professional therapist introducing caller
jokes.punchline: Brutally frank and unprofessional characterization
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of therapeutic professionalism

jokes.text: Hey, turn it up. I love hearing those wackos.
jokes.setup: Homer listening to therapy radio show
jokes.punchline: Homer enjoying others' problems while his wife is the caller
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony and character blindness

jokes.text: and he ate with utensils
jokes.setup: Marge listing ways Homer has deteriorated
jokes.punchline: Final item reveals how low standards have fallen
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation ending with absurdly basic expectation

jokes.text: Isn't that your wife, Homer? / Don't be ridiculous. My wife worships the ground I walk on.
jokes.setup: Barney recognizing Marge's voice on radio
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete delusion about his marriage
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-driven dramatic irony

jokes.text: The pig has made you into his mother. You are not the hot love object you deserve to be.
jokes.setup: Therapy advice being given
jokes.punchline: Harsh psychological truth delivered bluntly
jokes.explanation: Sharp but somewhat obvious relationship commentary

jokes.text: I'm as sure of it as I'm sure my voice is annoying.
jokes.setup: Dr. Monroe expressing certainty about his advice
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission about his grating voice
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor that breaks the fourth wall effectively

jokes.text: Leave Homer? / Don't use his real name! / Leave Pedro?
jokes.setup: Dr. Monroe trying to maintain caller anonymity
jokes.punchline: Marge's random substitution of 'Pedro'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd moment showing Marge's innocent confusion

jokes.text: Al Coholic prank call sequence
jokes.setup: Bart calls Moe's asking for 'Al Coholic'
jokes.punchline: Moe yells for 'Al Coholic' to the bar, realizes he's been tricked
jokes.explanation: Classic prank call format, became iconic Simpsons running gag

jokes.text: Hello. Is Al there? / Al? / Yeah, Al. Last name, Coholic.
jokes.setup: Bart making prank call asking for someone named Al
jokes.punchline: Last name reveals it's 'Alcoholic'
jokes.explanation: Classic prank call structure, well-executed if predictable

jokes.text: Wait a minute. Listen, you little yellow-bellied rat jackass. If I ever find out who you are, I'll kill ya!
jokes.setup: Moe realizes he's been pranked
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top violent threat with creative insults
jokes.explanation: Good character moment establishing Moe's volatile nature

jokes.text: I hope you do find that punk someday, Moe. Fill 'er up.
jokes.setup: Moe's violent threat about finding the prank caller
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious support while his son is the culprit
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony with Homer's characteristic obliviousness

jokes.text: Hey, Barney, am I a pig? / You're no more of a pig than I am. / Oh, no! / See? You're a pig.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Barney for reassurance about being a pig
jokes.punchline: Barney's comparison actually confirms Homer IS a pig
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect character logic

jokes.text: Barney's a pig. Larry's a pig. We're all pigs. Except for one difference. Once in a while, we can crawl out of the slop, hose ourselves off and act like human beings.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining the nature of men being pigs
jokes.punchline: Vivid barnyard metaphor for basic human decency
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly philosophical moment from Moe with great imagery

jokes.text: Gee, a romantic evening. Nah, she's too smart to fall for that.
jokes.setup: Moe suggesting Homer take Marge out for romance
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards logic about romance being manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's clueless approach to marriage

jokes.text: I'd like some flowers. / What kind of flowers? / You know, pretty ones, not dead.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to buy flowers
jokes.punchline: His hilariously basic criteria for flower selection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - childlike simplicity about adult tasks

jokes.text: We have some beautiful long-stem roses. They're $55 a dozen. / One, please.
jokes.setup: Expensive roses being offered by the dozen
jokes.punchline: Homer's cheapness leading to buying just one rose
jokes.explanation: Predictable but character-appropriate cheapskate humor

jokes.text: Your husband sees you as nothing, as nothing. The pig has made you into his mother, his mother.
jokes.setup: Marge hearing therapy advice replaying in her head
jokes.punchline: Repetitive echo effect making it more psychologically damaging
jokes.explanation: Effective psychological torment sequence, slightly overwrought

jokes.text: Marge, I, uh-- love you. No, that's not good. Marge, honey, I love you. Oh. Um-- Marge, I loveya, baby.
jokes.setup: Homer practicing what to say to Marge
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempts to find the right romantic tone
jokes.explanation: Homer's social awkwardness and inexperience with genuine emotion

jokes.text: Marge, sweetie, 'hooney,' honey-- Ah, this'll never work.
jokes.setup: Homer practicing romantic words to say to Marge
jokes.punchline: His mispronunciation and immediate defeat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's romantic ineptitude

jokes.text: Is Oliver there? / Who? / Oliver Klozoff.
jokes.setup: Another Bart prank call to Moe
jokes.punchline: 'Oliver Klozoff' sounds like 'all of her clothes off'
jokes.explanation: Classic prank call format, slightly more risqué than the first

jokes.text: It matters not, mon frere.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to be sophisticated about expensive restaurant
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at French sophistication
jokes.explanation: Good character moment of Homer's pretentious phase

jokes.text: Goodness. Must be a crossed wire.
jokes.setup: Moe's violent threat bleeding into their phone conversation
jokes.punchline: Marge's innocent explanation for the disturbing interruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge understatement in face of obvious violence

jokes.text: The Simpsons. Lady, you've gotta be kidding!
jokes.setup: Marge calling for a babysitter
jokes.punchline: The Simpson family's notorious reputation
jokes.explanation: Good establishment of the family's chaotic reputation

jokes.text: Hello. This is Mr. S-Sampson. / Did your wife just call a second ago? / No, I said Sampson, not Simpson.
jokes.setup: Homer calling same service with fake name
jokes.punchline: Transparent attempt to hide identity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the babysitter problem with Homer's ineptitude

jokes.text: Those Simpsons, what a bunch of savages! Especially that big-ape father. / Doh!
jokes.setup: Service complaining about the Simpson father
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation revealing he heard it all
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff with Homer's iconic catchphrase

jokes.text: Actually, the Simpsons are neighbors of ours, and we've found them to be a quite misunderstood and underrated family.
jokes.setup: Homer caught listening to insults about his family
jokes.punchline: His attempt to defend them while maintaining the fake identity
jokes.explanation: Homer's clumsy attempt to save face while defending his family

jokes.text: Dad dances? / Like an angel.
jokes.setup: Lisa's skeptical question about Homer dancing
jokes.punchline: Marge's loving but obviously false assessment
jokes.explanation: Good marital dynamic showing Marge's protective delusions

jokes.text: Son, there's not a woman alive who can resist a man who knows how to mambo. / You don't have a clue, do you, Dad?
jokes.setup: Homer dispensing dating wisdom about dancing
jokes.punchline: Bart's direct assessment of Homer's cluelessness
jokes.explanation: Good father-son dynamic with Bart's brutal honesty

jokes.text: Smooth as a baby's behind!
jokes.setup: Homer admiring himself after shaving
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately colorful metaphor for being clean-shaven
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor with Homer's crude comparisons

jokes.text: Oh, boy! The Happy Little Elves Meet the Curious Bear Cub. / Oh, the Elves! The Elves!
jokes.setup: Bart sarcastically excited about children's video
jokes.punchline: Lisa's genuine but over-the-top enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic showing their different personalities

jokes.text: Bye, kids! Watch out for the boy.
jokes.setup: Parents leaving for romantic evening
jokes.punchline: Marge's warning about Bart to the babysitter
jokes.explanation: Marge's motherly concern about Bart's troublemaking

jokes.text: And I don't know what that is, because everybody has always done what I say.
jokes.setup: Miss Botz threatening the children
jokes.punchline: Admitting she's never had to follow through on threats
jokes.explanation: Menacing but incompetent threat reveals character's inexperience

jokes.text: Well, when you choose one that's floating upside down, it somewhat defeats the purpose of selecting a live lobster.
jokes.setup: Homer selecting a dead lobster from tank
jokes.punchline: Waiter's polite but pointed correction
jokes.explanation: Good service industry politeness masking obvious stupidity

jokes.text: We've seen the Crappy Little Elves about 14 billion times.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about watching the children's video
jokes.punchline: Deliberate mispronunciation to 'Crappy' and hyperbolic count
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart rebellion with good wordplay

jokes.text: You know what happens. They find Captain Kook's treasure. All the elves dance around like idiots. I puke. The end.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why he doesn't want to watch the video
jokes.punchline: Cynical plot summary ending with his physical reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart cynicism with escalating disdain

jokes.text: Bart, you're just like Chilly, the elf who cannot love.
jokes.setup: Lisa responding to Bart's cynicism
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to specific character from the show with dramatic trait
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lisa moment - earnest but absurd children's show reference

jokes.text: Relax. This is cinema verite. When the brutal, slow-motion killing starts, I'll tell you to shut your eyes.
jokes.setup: Lisa worried about watching violent show
jokes.punchline: Bart's pretentious film term paired with graphic content description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment combining sophistication with inappropriate content

jokes.text: 'Cause I'm so care free? / No, because you're wearing a bib.
jokes.setup: Homer asking why he looks like a little boy
jokes.punchline: The literal reason rather than personality trait
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect economy

jokes.text: Garcon! Another bottle of your second-least-expensive champagne.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to be sophisticated at fancy restaurant
jokes.punchline: Revealing his cheapness while attempting elegance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - sophisticated pretension undermined by cheapness

jokes.text: Except for one thing-- no chaperone.
jokes.setup: Homer comparing current date to when they were dating
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate focus on lack of supervision
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Homer's inappropriate mindset

jokes.text: Remember, she may be using a clever alias... / Botz!
jokes.setup: TV warning about criminal using aliases
jokes.punchline: The 'clever' alias is transparently obvious
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony with obvious criminal alias

jokes.text: Don't forget to tell me when you see the Offramp. / Oh, there it-- went. / No problemo. We'll just get off at the next exit.
jokes.setup: Marge reminding Homer to watch for their exit
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate failure and casual attitude
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment showing his driving incompetence and casual attitude

jokes.text: Seeing as no one was hurt, I think it would be really silly to dwell on this.
jokes.setup: Criminal babysitter has tied up children
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of kidnapping as minor incident
jokes.explanation: Good villainous understatement of serious crimes

jokes.text: You're a smart, young man, Bart. I hope you're smart enough to keep your mouth shut. / He isn't.
jokes.setup: Criminal complimenting Bart's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact assessment of her brother
jokes.explanation: Excellent sibling dynamic with Lisa's brutal honesty about Bart

jokes.text: Stupid Sampsons.
jokes.setup: Criminal going through their belongings
jokes.punchline: Still using the fake name from earlier phone call
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing the fake name stuck

jokes.text: Homer, would it spoil the mood if I called home, you know, just to check on the kids?
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge in romantic motel setting
jokes.punchline: Marge's maternal worry interrupting romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect motherly instinct vs romantic timing conflict

jokes.text: Ask if there's a reward. / Is there a reward? / If she's convicted, we get T-shirts! / Yeah!
jokes.setup: Kids asking about reward for catching criminal
jokes.punchline: Pathetically small reward of just T-shirts
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax subverting expectation of meaningful reward

jokes.text: Don't turn your back on that boy for a second. / Ain't that the truth? You know, one time he-- / Huh?
jokes.setup: Criminal warning about Bart being dangerous
jokes.punchline: Homer agreeing then realizing she's escaping
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness moment with delayed realization

jokes.text: Oh, uh, are you sure this microphone works? Uh, well, I wouldn't say I aided her. This is on, right?
jokes.setup: Homer being interviewed on TV news
jokes.punchline: His paranoid questions about being broadcast live
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's media anxiety

jokes.text: Have you ever seen a kung fu movie? It was just like that. But now I know her moves. So, if you're listening to me, lady, you'd better think long and hard before tying something like this on Homer Simpson again!
jokes.setup: Homer describing his 'struggle' with the babysitter
jokes.punchline: Absurdly heroic retelling and threatening the escaped criminal on TV
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer delusion and bravado escalating to direct criminal challenge

jokes.text: Lord, help me. I'm just not that bright.
jokes.setup: Homer's realization after his TV appearance
jokes.punchline: Rare moment of self-awareness about his intelligence
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly self-aware moment for Homer, well-executed character beat

jokes.text: The way I see it, if you raise three children who can knock out and hogtie a perfect stranger, you must be doing something right.
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on the night's events
jokes.punchline: Finding positive parenting lesson in children's violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect twisted Homer logic turning violence into parenting success

jokes.text: The way I see it, if you raise three children who can knock out and hogtie a perfect stranger, you must be doing something right.
jokes.setup: Homer feeling bad about his parenting
jokes.punchline: Marge finding positive spin on children's violent capabilities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant twisted parenting logic and perfect episode closure
